soap:
REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7464 — perf(index): coalesce concurrent scalar-index opens (single-flight)
Ali2Arslan (first_time) · L (+184/-21, 2 files) · no escalation

SUBJECTIVE (what the PR claims)
  On a cold `open_scalar_index`, every concurrent caller independently re-reads
  `page_lookup.lance` and rebuilds the lookup — an N× I/O + transient-memory
  spike. The PR adds a single-flight cache primitive
  (`LanceCache::get_or_insert_unsized_with_key`, the `Arc<dyn Trait>` analogue of
  the existing sized `get_or_insert_with_key`) and routes the scalar-index open
  through it, so racing cold opens share one load. Warm open returns the live
  index with no I/O. Author asserts behavior is otherwise unchanged.

OBJECTIVE (ground truth, verified at head 7fef56a)
  • rust/lance-core/src/cache/mod.rs:435 — new `get_or_insert_unsized_with_key`.
    Wraps loader result in `Arc::new(value)` → `Arc<Arc<dyn Trait>>` and
    downcasts `entry.downcast::<Arc<K::ValueType>>()`. This Arc-nesting EXACTLY
    matches `insert_unsized_with_key` (mod.rs:599: `Arc::new(value)`) and
    `get_unsized_with_key` (mod.rs:591: `downcast::<Arc<K::ValueType>>`). So the
    new method and the default `put_in_cache`/`get_from_cache` agree on layout —
    no downcast-type mismatch. Verified, not assumed.
  • Single-flight backend contract — rust/lance-core/src/cache/moka.rs:74
    `get_or_insert` uses moka `optionally_get_with`, which runs `init` at most
    once per full key under concurrency. On loader error it returns `None`,
    propagates the error via a oneshot, and does NOT cache the entry. So the new
    method's doc claim ("a failing loader is not cached … next call retries") is
    backed by the backend. Verified.
  • Cache-key uniqueness — `ScalarIndexCacheKey.key()` is the constant
    `"scalar_index"` (registry.rs:230-244). It is safe because the caller derives
    a per-index-namespaced cache: scalar.rs:447
    `index_cache = dataset.index_cache.for_index(&index.uuid, frag_reuse uuid)`,
    and `build_key` (mod.rs:150) prepends `self.prefix`. Different indices → different
    prefix → different full key. Constant key does NOT collide across indices.
    This is the same key the pre-existing in-memory path already used.
  • The renamed binding is correct — scalar.rs:496 tracing line reads
    `index_uuid = %index.uuid` (the `&IndexMetadata` param, still in scope inside
    the `move` closure) and `index_type = opened.index_type()` (the loaded index).
    The deleted `let index_uuid = index.uuid;` is harmless: `index` is still
    captured. No dangling/renamed-variable bug.
  • Tests genuinely exercise single-flight, not just happy path:
    - cache `test_get_or_insert_unsized_dedup` (mod.rs:1073): 5 racing callers, a
      `yield_now()` inside the loader so losers register as waiters before the
      winner finishes, asserts `load_count == 1`. Sound.
    - lance `test_open_scalar_index_coalesces_concurrent_cold_opens`
      (scalar.rs:1788): builds a BTree index, REOPENS the dataset so the index
      cache is cold, fires 8 concurrent `open_scalar_index`, asserts all 8 return
      and `metrics.index_loads == 1`. Directly proves coalescing. Sound.
  • CI green, mergeable=true; PR body reports `cargo fmt` + `clippy -p lance-core
    -p lance --tests` clean. The trait bounds (`dyn ScalarIndex: DeepSizeOf +
    Send + Sync + 'static`) compile (CI), so the generic is satisfiable.
  • Could not read: none — both changed files read in full, 0 omitted, no patch
    truncation.

ASSESSMENT
  risk: med (refined from baseline med, held) · urgency: high
  Rationale: pure concurrency/caching change on a hot read path. The two failure
  modes that would make this dangerous — (a) a wrong/colliding cache key
  returning the wrong index, and (b) a poisoned entry caching a transient I/O
  failure — were both checked against ground truth and are handled correctly
  (per-index namespaced prefix; moka does not cache loader errors). The change
  faithfully mirrors an already-shipped sized primitive. No `unsafe`, no
  format/wire change, no API break (new pub method is additive). Risk does not
  rise to high.
  escalation: none (no forced rules)

  Non-gating observations (NOT conditions):
  • Per-caller metrics under coalescing: only the winner's loader runs, so only
    the winner's `MetricsCollector` records `record_index_load()`; the 7 coalesced
    losers record 0. This is DEFENSIBLE — the losers performed no I/O, and the
    pre-existing warm-hit path likewise recorded 0. It is a deliberate semantic
    ("record_index_load == an actual load IOP happened"), and the test documents
    it. Flagging for visibility, not as a defect.
  • Redundant in-memory double-store for the DEFAULT plugin: inside the loader,
    `put_in_cache` (→ `insert_unsized_with_key` under "scalar_index") writes the
    index, and then `get_or_insert` stores the same `Arc` under the same key.
    Idempotent (same value, last-write-wins). The explicit `put_in_cache` is still
    needed for plugins that override it to persist a serializable form under a
    different sized key, so it is correct to keep — just redundant for the default.
  • The single-flight coalesces the expensive part (`load_index` + the
    `page_lookup.lance` read, which the PR body names as the dominant cost). The
    pre-loader prelude (`LanceIndexStore::from_dataset_for_existing`,
    `fetch_index_details`, `open_frag_reuse_index`) still runs once per caller;
    `fetch_index_details` is in-memory/cached in the common path. The headline
    N× cost is addressed; the prelude is comparatively cheap. Scope is fine.

PLAN

  DRAFT COMMENTS (all for the attending to co-sign; nothing posted)
    question(non-blocking): Under concurrent cold-open FAILURE, the moka backend's
      oneshot error channel (moka.rs:~100) delivers the real error to only one
      racing caller; the others fall through to the generic
      "Failed to retrieve error from cache loader" internal error. This is
      pre-existing backend behavior you are now exercising on the index-open path.
      Intentional / acceptable for open_scalar_index? (Not gating — observability
      only, and it predates this PR.)
    suggestion(non-blocking): Consider a one-line comment at the loader's
      `put_in_cache` call noting it is kept for serializable-form plugins and is
      redundant for the default in-memory plugin (which `get_or_insert` already
      caches), so a future reader doesn't "simplify" it away.
    praise: The dedup test uses `yield_now()` to force waiter registration before
      the winner resolves — that is the difference between a test that proves
      single-flight and one that merely happens to pass. Nice.

RECOMMENDATION: approve — no blocking conditions.
  confidence: high. Cache-key namespacing, Arc-nesting, error-not-poisoned, and
  the renamed binding were each verified against source at the PR head; both
  tests genuinely exercise the concurrent path; CI green.
  what would make this wrong: if `for_index` did NOT actually fold the uuid into
  the cache prefix (then the constant "scalar_index" key would alias across
  indices and return the wrong index) — but that namespacing predates this PR and
  is the same mechanism the existing in-memory cache path already relied on, so a
  regression there would have surfaced before. Worth a 30-second confirm of
  `for_index`'s prefix construction if the attending wants belt-and-suspenders.
